```{r}

##################################
## Installing and loading packages 
##################################

# Install SNPRelate and gdsfmt 
library(devtools)
if (!requireNamespace("SNPRelate", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install_github("zhengxwen/gdsfmt")
  install_github("zhengxwen/SNPRelate")
}

# Load required packages
library(SNPRelate)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)    # For color palettes
library(dplyr)      # Data manipulation
library(MASS)       # Matrix operations
library(corrplot)   # Correlation matrix plot
library(RColorBrewer)  # Color palettes

```

```{r}

#############################################
## Prepare genotypic data and create GDS file
#############################################

## Across all populations
# Load genotypic matrix from a CSV file
geno_matrix <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/All pops/All pops_filtered.csv", 
  row.names = 1, 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Remove REF and ALT columns
geno_matrix <- geno_matrix[, !(colnames(geno_matrix) %in% c("REF", "ALT"))]

# Convert the matrix to a proper format
geno_matrix <- as.matrix(geno_matrix)

# Create GDS file
snpgdsCreateGeno(
  gds.fn = "genotype.gds",        # Output GDS file name
  genmat = geno_matrix,           # Genotypic matrix
  sample.id = colnames(geno_matrix),  # Sample IDs
  snp.id = rownames(geno_matrix),     # SNP IDs
  snpfirstdim = TRUE              # SNPs as rows, individuals as columns
)

## Within subsets of populations based on recurrent parent
# Load genotype matrix for KS061406LN.26 populations (Pop30, Pop32, Pop34)
geno_matrix_LN26 <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/All pops/KS061406LN.26 pops_filtered.csv",
  row.names = 1, 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
geno_matrix_LN26 <- as.matrix(geno_matrix_LN26)  # Convert to matrix format for further processing

# Create a GDS file for the KS061406LN.26 populations
snpgdsCreateGeno(
  gds.fn = "genotype_LN26.gds",  # Output GDS file name
  genmat = geno_matrix_LN26,     # Input genotypic matrix
  sample.id = colnames(geno_matrix_LN26),  # Use column names as sample IDs
  snp.id = rownames(geno_matrix_LN26),    # Use row names as SNP IDs
  snpfirstdim = TRUE                      # SNPs are rows, individuals are columns
)

# Load genotype matrix for KS061278M.4 populations (Pop41, Pop42, Pop46, Pop80)
geno_matrix_M4 <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/All pops/KS061278M.4 pops_filtered.csv",
  row.names = 1, 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
geno_matrix_M4 <- as.matrix(geno_matrix_M4)  # Convert to matrix format

# Create a GDS file for KS061278M.4 populations
snpgdsCreateGeno(
  gds.fn = "genotype_M4.gds",  # Output GDS file
  genmat = geno_matrix_M4,     # Input genotypic matrix
  sample.id = colnames(geno_matrix_M4),  # Sample IDs
  snp.id = rownames(geno_matrix_M4),    # SNP IDs
  snpfirstdim = TRUE                    # SNPs as rows, individuals as columns
)

```

```{r}

##############
## Perform PCA
##############

## Across populations
# Open the GDS file
genofile <- snpgdsOpen("genotype.gds")

# PCA on all SNPs
pca_result <- snpgdsPCA(genofile, num.thread = 2)
pc.percent <- pca_result$varprop * 100  # Convert to percentage

# Create a PCA data frame
pca_data <- data.frame(Sample = pca_result$sample.id, PC1 = pca_result$eigenvect[,1], PC2 = pca_result$eigenvect[,2])

# Save PCA results to a CSV file
write.csv(pca_data, "PCA_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

## Within groups of populations based on recurrent parent
# PCA for KS061406LN.26 populations (Pop30, Pop32, Pop34)
genofile_LN26 <- snpgdsOpen("genotype_LN26.gds")  # Open the GDS file 
pca_result_LN26 <- snpgdsPCA(genofile_LN26, num.thread = 2)  # Perform PCA

# Calculate variance explained by each principal component
pc.percent_LN26 <- pca_result_LN26$varprop * 100  # Convert variance to percentage

# Create a data frame for PCA results (first two principal components)
pca_data_LN26 <- data.frame(
  Sample = pca_result_LN26$sample.id,  # Sample IDs
  PC1 = pca_result_LN26$eigenvect[, 1],  # PC1
  PC2 = pca_result_LN26$eigenvect[, 2]   # PC2
)
write.csv(pca_data_LN26, "PCA_results_LN26.csv", row.names = FALSE)  # Save PCA results

# PCA for KS061278M.4 populations (Pop41, Pop42, Pop46, Pop80)
genofile_M4 <- snpgdsOpen("genotype_M4.gds")  # Open the GDS file
pca_result_M4 <- snpgdsPCA(genofile_M4, num.thread = 2)  # Perform PCA

# Calculate variance explained by each principal component
pc.percent_M4 <- pca_result_M4$varprop * 100

# Create a data frame for PCA results
pca_data_M4 <- data.frame(
  Sample = pca_result_M4$sample.id,  # Sample IDs
  PC1 = pca_result_M4$eigenvect[, 1],  # PC1
  PC2 = pca_result_M4$eigenvect[, 2]   # PC2
)
write.csv(pca_data_M4, "PCA_results_M4.csv", row.names = FALSE)  # Save results

```

```{r}

####################################
## Load PCA data and create PC plots
####################################

## Across populations
# Load population-labeled PCA results
pca_data <- read.csv("PCA_results_population_labels.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Define custom population colors
population_colors <- c(
  "Pop30" = "#a6761d", "Pop32" = "#1b9e77", "Pop34" = "#d95f02",
  "Pop41" = "#7570b3", "Pop42" = "#e7298a", "Pop46" = "#66a61e", "Pop80" = "#e6ab02"
)

# Define shapes based on recurrent parent
parent_shapes <- c("KS061406LN~26" = 21, "KS061278M-4" = 24)

# Create PCA plot with color-coded populations and black outlines using shapes for parents
ggplot(pca_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = Population, shape = Recurrent.Parent)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, color = "black", stroke = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = population_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = parent_shapes) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA across 7 TdGD Populations",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(pc.percent[1], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], 1), "%)"),
    shape = "Recurrent Parent"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21)))

# Save the PCA plot to a PNG file
ggsave("PCA_combined.png", width = 8, height = 6, bg = "white")

## Within groups of populations based on recurrent parent
# Load PCA results for KS061406LN.26 populations (Pop30, Pop32, Pop34)
pca_data_LN26 <- read.csv("PCA_results_LN26_population_labels.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Define custom colors for the populations
population_colors_LN26 <- c(
  "Pop30" = "#a6761d",
  "Pop32" = "#1b9e77",
  "Pop34" = "#d95f02"
)

# Create the PCA plot for KS061406LN~26 populations
pca_plot_LN26 <- ggplot(
  pca_data_LN26,
  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = Population)
) +
  geom_point(
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.5,
    alpha = 0.8,
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = population_colors_LN26) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA for KS061406LN~26 Populations",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(pc.percent_LN26[1], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(pc.percent_LN26[2], 1), "%)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Save the plot as a PNG file
ggsave("PCA_plot_LN26.png", plot = pca_plot_LN26, width = 8, height = 6, bg = "white")

# Load PCA results for KS061278M.4 populations (Pop41, Pop42, Pop46, Pop80)
pca_data_M4 <- read.csv("PCA_results_M4_population_labels.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Define custom colors for the populations
population_colors_M4 <- c(
  "Pop41" = "#7570b3",
  "Pop42" = "#e7298a",
  "Pop46" = "#66a61e",
  "Pop80" = "#e6ab02"
)

# Create the PCA plot for KS061278M-4 populations
pca_plot_M4 <- ggplot(
  pca_data_M4,
  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = Population)
) +
  geom_point(
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.5,
    alpha = 0.8,
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.4
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = population_colors_M4) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA for KS061278M-4 Populations",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(pc.percent_M4[1], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(pc.percent_M4[2], 1), "%)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Save the plot as a PNG file
ggsave("PCA_plot_M4.png", plot = pca_plot_M4, width = 8, height = 6, bg = "white")

```

```{r}

########################
## SNP correlation plots
########################

## Across populations
# Read the genetic map file
gm <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/PCA/Genetic map.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Extract SNP IDs from the GDS file to ensure alignment
snp_ids <- read.gdsn(index.gdsn(genofile, "snp.id"))

# Match the genetic map to the SNP IDs in the GDS file
gm <- gm[match(snp_ids, gm$SNP.ID), ]

# Check for missing chromosome entries in the genetic map
chr <- gm$Chromosome
if (any(is.na(chr))) {
  stop("Some SNPs are not matched with the genetic map.")
}

# Split chromosomes into groups A, B, and D based on their names
chr_group <- gsub("\\d", "", chr)  # Extract A/B/D group from chromosome name

# Assign different colors to A, B, D using viridis magma palette
group_A_colors <- "#7570b3"
group_B_colors <- "#1b9e77"
group_D_colors <- "#d95f02"

# Assign colors to each SNP based on chromosome group
colors_for_chr <- sapply(chr_group, function(group) {
  if (group == "A") return(group_A_colors)
  if (group == "B") return(group_B_colors)
  if (group == "D") return(group_D_colors)
})

# Calculate SNP correlations with PC1 and PC2
CORR <- snpgdsPCACorr(pca_result, genofile, eig.which = 1:4)

# Get chromosome boundaries for plotting
chr_boundaries <- cumsum(table(chr))

# Calculate midpoints of chromosomes for labeling purposes
chr_midpoints <- (c(0, chr_boundaries[-length(chr_boundaries)]) + chr_boundaries) / 2
chr_labels <- unique(chr)  # Chromosome names

# Plot SNP correlation with principal components
png("SNP_correlation_plots.png", width = 800, height = 600)

# Adjust the plot layout and margins
par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mai = c(1, 0.8, 0.6, 0.5))  # Adjust bottom margin for x-axis labels

# Loop over the first two principal components to plot SNP correlations
for (i in 1:2) {
  # Plot absolute SNP correlations with PC1 and PC2, using chromosome colors
  plot(abs(CORR$snpcorr[i, ]), ylim = c(0, 1), xlab = "Chromosome", 
       ylab = "Correlation", col = colors_for_chr, pch = "+", xaxt = "n")
  
  # Add chromosome labels at the calculated midpoints
  axis(1, at = chr_midpoints, labels = chr_labels, tick = FALSE, las = 1, cex.axis = 0.8)
  
  # Add titles to the plot for each PC
  title(main = paste("SNP Correlation with Principal Component", i), line = 1.5, cex.main = 1.2)
}

# Save the plot and close the PNG device
dev.off()


## Within groups of populations based on recurrent parent and within each population

gds_LN26   <- "genotype_LN26.gds"
gds_M4     <- "genotype_M4.gds"
labels_csv <- "PCA_results_population_labels.csv"   # must have Sample, Population
gm_csv     <- "Genetic map.csv"                     # must have SNP.ID, Chromosome

out_dir <- "SNP_PCcorr_plots"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Read labels and genetic map
lab <- read.csv(labels_csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
stopifnot(all(c("Sample", "Population") %in% names(lab)))
lab$Sample <- trimws(lab$Sample)
lab$Population <- trimws(lab$Population)

gm <- read.csv(gm_csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
stopifnot(all(c("SNP.ID", "Chromosome") %in% names(gm)))
gm$SNP.ID <- trimws(gm$SNP.ID)
gm$Chromosome <- trimws(gm$Chromosome)

# Define RP groups
LN26_pops <- c("Pop30", "Pop32", "Pop34")
M4_pops   <- c("Pop41", "Pop42", "Pop46", "Pop80")

# Helper function
plot_snp_pcorr_old <- function(genofile, gm, title_prefix, out_png,
                               sample_ids = NULL,
                               pcs_to_plot = 1:2,
                               num_thread = 2) {

  gds_samples <- read.gdsn(index.gdsn(genofile, "sample.id"))
  if (!is.null(sample_ids)) {
    sample_ids <- intersect(sample_ids, gds_samples)
    if (length(sample_ids) < 3) stop("Too few samples for: ", title_prefix)
  }

  # PCA
  pca <- snpgdsPCA(genofile, sample.id = sample_ids, num.thread = num_thread)

  # Correlations
  corr <- snpgdsPCACorr(pca, genofile, eig.which = pcs_to_plot)

  snp_ids <- read.gdsn(index.gdsn(genofile, "snp.id"))
  snp_ids <- as.character(snp_ids)

  # Map SNP -> Chromosome
  gm_sub <- gm[match(snp_ids, gm$SNP.ID), , drop = FALSE]
  chr <- gm_sub$Chromosome
  if (any(is.na(chr))) {
    bad <- snp_ids[is.na(chr)]
    stop("Genetic map missing Chromosome for some SNPs in: ", title_prefix,
         "\nExample missing SNP IDs: ", paste(head(bad, 10), collapse = ", "))
  }

  # Color by subgenome (A/B/D)
  chr_group <- gsub("\\d", "", chr)
  group_A_color <- "#7570b3"
  group_B_color <- "#1b9e77"
  group_D_color <- "#d95f02"
  colors_for_chr <- ifelse(chr_group == "A", group_A_color,
                    ifelse(chr_group == "B", group_B_color, group_D_color))

  # Chromosome boundaries/midpoints
  chr_boundaries <- cumsum(table(chr))
  chr_midpoints  <- (c(0, chr_boundaries[-length(chr_boundaries)]) + chr_boundaries) / 2
  chr_labels     <- unique(chr)

  # Corr columns should match SNP count
  if (ncol(corr$snpcorr) != length(snp_ids)) {
    stop("Mismatch: corr has ", ncol(corr$snpcorr),
         " SNPs but GDS has ", length(snp_ids),
         " for: ", title_prefix,
         "\n(This usually means PCACorr did not run on all SNPs somehow.)")
  }

  # Plot
  png(out_png, width = 900, height = 650)
  par(mfrow = c(length(pcs_to_plot), 1), mai = c(1, 0.8, 0.8, 0.3))

  for (k in seq_along(pcs_to_plot)) {
    pc_i <- pcs_to_plot[k]
    y <- abs(corr$snpcorr[k, ])

    plot(y,
         ylim = c(0, 1),
         xlab = "Chromosome",
         ylab = "Correlation",
         col = colors_for_chr,
         pch = "+",
         xaxt = "n")

    axis(1, at = chr_midpoints, labels = chr_labels, tick = FALSE, las = 1, cex.axis = 0.8)

    title(main = paste0(title_prefix, ": SNP correlation with PC", pc_i),
          line = 1.5, cex.main = 1.1)
  }

  dev.off()
}

# Group plots
# LN26 group (Pop30/32/34)
plot_snp_pcorr_old(
  genofile     = genofile_LN26,
  gm           = gm,
  title_prefix = "LN26 group (Pop30/32/34)",
  out_png      = file.path(out_dir, "SNP_correlation_LN26_group_PC1_PC2.png"),
  sample_ids   = NULL,
  pcs_to_plot  = 1:2,
  num_thread   = 2
)

# M4 group (Pop41/42/46/80)
plot_snp_pcorr_old(
  genofile     = genofile_M4,
  gm           = gm,
  title_prefix = "M-4 group (Pop41/42/46/80)",
  out_png      = file.path(out_dir, "SNP_correlation_M4_group_PC1_PC2.png"),
  sample_ids   = NULL,
  pcs_to_plot  = 1:2,
  num_thread   = 2
)

# Per-population plots
for (pop in LN26_pops) {
  pop_ids <- lab$Sample[lab$Population == pop]
  plot_snp_pcorr_old(
    genofile     = genofile_LN26,
    gm           = gm,
    title_prefix = pop,
    out_png      = file.path(out_dir, paste0("SNP_correlation_", pop, "_PC1_PC2.png")),
    sample_ids   = pop_ids,
    pcs_to_plot  = 1:2,
    num_thread   = 2
  )
}

for (pop in M4_pops) {
  pop_ids <- lab$Sample[lab$Population == pop]
  plot_snp_pcorr_old(
    genofile     = genofile_M4,
    gm           = gm,
    title_prefix = pop,
    out_png      = file.path(out_dir, paste0("SNP_correlation_", pop, "_PC1_PC2.png")),
    sample_ids   = pop_ids,
    pcs_to_plot  = 1:2,
    num_thread   = 2
  )
}

# Close
snpgdsClose(genofile_LN26)
snpgdsClose(genofile_M4)

cat("Done. Plots written to: ", normalizePath(out_dir), "\n")

```

```{r}

########################################
## Investigation of structuring in Pop30
########################################

# Vrn

pca <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/PCA/Vrn_Rht_Pop30/Pop30_Vrn_Rht.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pca$Vrn.A1 <- factor(pca$`Vrn.A1`, levels = c(0, 1, 2))

vrn_cols <- c(
  "0" = "#1b9e77",
  "2" = "#d95f02",
  "1" = "#7570b3"
)

pca <- pca |>
  subset(!is.na(Vrn.A1) & !is.na(PC1) & !is.na(PC2))


p_vrn <- ggplot(pca, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = Vrn.A1)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, color = "black", stroke = 0.4, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = vrn_cols, name = "Vrn-A1") +
  labs(
    title = "PCA (colored by Vrn-A1)",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_vrn

ggsave("PCA_plot_Pop30_Vrn.A1.png", plot = p_vrn, width = 8, height = 6, bg = "white")

# D genome donor proportion

p_donD <- ggplot(pca, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = DonorPropD)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "YlOrBr",
    direction = 1,
    name = "DonorProp (D)"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA (colored by donor proportion)",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# Genome wide donor proportion

p_don <- ggplot(pca, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = DonorProp)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "YlOrBr",
    direction = 1,
    name = "DonorProp"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA (colored by donor proportion)",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_donD
p_don

ggsave("PCA_plot_Pop30_DonorPropD.png", plot = p_donD, width = 8, height = 6, bg = "white")
ggsave("PCA_plot_Pop30_DonorProp.png", plot = p_don, width = 8, height = 6, bg = "white")

```

```{r}

########################################################################
## Investigation of population structuring correlated with chromosome 2A
########################################################################

# 2NS

pca <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/PCA/2NS_Pop80/PCA_results_M4_investigate.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

pca <- subset(pca, !is.na(X2NS) & X2NS !="")

X2NS_cols <- c(
  "2NS" = "#1b9e77",
  "non 2NS" = "#d95f02",
  "Het" = "#7570b3"
)

p_2NS <- ggplot(pca, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = X2NS)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = X2NS_cols, name = "2NS") +
  labs(
    title = "PCA (colored by 2NS)",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_2NS

# 2A donor proportions

p_donprop <- ggplot(pca, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = DonorProp_2A)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "YlOrBr",
    direction = 1,
    name = "DonorProp (2A)"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA (colored by donor proportions)",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_donprop

```
